Kit that I could use to deploy to someone, right?

you.

by using our products.

Line item in any IT budget, whether you're MSP, mid-market, enterprise.

IT space, that's, that's what it is.

would say, so, so, platforms, so, MDR, the security space, and the management space is extremely crowded.

But the way they evaluate usually is in blocks, depending on the tool.
But they need all of it.
Now, we might see that, and maybe some leanings, right?
Walking into, because the UEM solution is that major control panel, major control plane for a managed service provider, right?
It's the arms and legs of a managed service provider, right?
You need to do things.
You go to your RMM to make it happen, right?
You go to your UEM to make it happen.
Getting in and displacing that is difficult.
However, walking in with something like Cove saying, hey, we've got a much better backup solution for you.
We can save you lots of time and energy.
Like MSPs are eating that up, right?
And so I think that's an angle like the MSPs can go into Cove and walk in really easily and just flip a product over to us.
Whereas the UEM is a little harder.
And us going, the UEM going into the mid-market space more is also an easier one, right?
So all of a sudden, that ocean isn't as red as it is in the MSP space.
So we're finding it a little easier to do stuff.
And in fact, we have not focused on mid-market from a UEM perspective, let's say, with critical eye and priority.
However, it's producing, right?
So on its own is a mid-market.
With the UEM is doing extremely well.
I think our AdLumen solution has been largely channel driven.
And because it is a service, right?
It doesn't matter whether we're selling it to the MSP and we're providing that service or it's mid-market.
It's done very well in mid-market right through the channel.
So that's another kind of split where we see a little bit of an advantage.
It's not like we purposely do that, let's say.
Like, we're out trying to get everybody.
The UEM is trying to get everybody.
However, what we found is that, you know, those displacements seem to work a little easier depending on those markets.
When you say channel driven, you mean MSP channel?
No.
Well, let's say we mean, you know, distribution, resellers, bars, and MSPs having a bar moment.
Right?
you next time.
Gotcha.
All right.
Is there any specific customer stories you guys anchor on, or is there a kind of recent one that shows, like, big impact across the portfolio of products?
Just trying to get an understanding of, like, There is one we've got recently.
I'm not sure if it's going to be, like, like an anchor story, though, to your point, but it does show all three products, I think, or at least two out of three, if I remember correctly.
It's with a pretty big MSP, a pretty important customer of ours.
So we can find that one for you once it gets mocked up, but it's, like, brand new one.
Are you talking about the Joyce one?
Yeah.
Yeah, I think Jay's almost done with that, if not done.
Yeah.
Any others that you guys historically highlight?
Yeah, it's an interesting question about whether it's across all the products.
We tend to, even if they tell a story across all the products, they might be leaning towards one or another, and we pull out that story.
But, yeah, I'm trying to think.
You need to magnify.
Was that one out yet?
I don't know.
I don't know of that one.

When you're kind of in the field or talking.
Talking to customers or at conferences, are there, you know, themes or phrases that are consistently landing with customers?
So, like, talking about automation or the cyber resiliency, what kind of stories are just kind of getting people, like, head nodding?
Like, that get people's head nodding?
Yeah, I feel like if I'm out in field, right, like, yeah, I think if we're out in the field, it's a lot about, like, what their pain points are today, right?

Like, people aren't, like, out there wanting to listen to stuff or shopping or even interested in hearing a whole lot unless it relates to a specific pain point that they might be facing with their business today.
Our hope is that the cyber resilience message, as it's brand new, is the one that, like, that's the plan, right?
Right.

That is going to be the one that end-to-end cyber resilience is being the message that really resonates with the marketplace, being the only vendor that, as Joe, you know, highlighted there, best in class across the before, during, and after a cyber attack.
Yeah, I would say it also depends on, you know, which tool the customer is looking at.

If someone's coming to Cove, a lot of things that are, you know, important to them and stories we talk about is how much time they're going to save if they switch to Cove based off of their current setup.

So time savings is a big one, you know, support is a really big one, reliability of the products, and security of the product is a big one, because today they may have a Frankenstein setup that leaves them vulnerable.
A lot of times too, like labor costs and savings, you know, a lot of these.
So MSPs have like tier three techs that are working on, and there's a massive talent shortage in the market as well.

So they have these tier three techs managing all these solutions and, you know, working on all this stuff day to day, whereas with they come to consolidate things in our, at Enable, they can let, that tech now can focus on other projects that are more important to the business and they can have their tier one techs actually manage those things that that tier three tech was.
So there's, I don't know, there's a, there's a few things, but, but yeah.
Sure.
Helpful.
Thanks, Brittany.
All right.
So I think I've got at least enough for like sales and kind of customer insights, some proof, some product value.
I guess if you were kind of switched into like the tone and voice of Enable.
Yeah.
What are maybe some metaphors that best describe an Able's personality that you guys kind of go to market with?
It's really tough to say.
I feel like in the top for the most part.
Yeah, go ahead.
Sorry.
I mean, this is me coming as an outsider in recently, so I'm new to the organization.
And there's been a shift in the brand messaging since then.
So when I was first joining or when I did my research, right, like it was very much like empowering MSPs and IT teams, kind of this fun sort of startup vibe.

I think we're trying to shift away from that and be more serious as a company and like really talk more about that cybersecurity angle and like maybe enable, like in the past, yeah, we're enabling you to be successful.
We still are, but I think it has more of a serious, more serious connotation to it than this kind of fun hippie vibe.
And I wouldn't, I don't know if I'd use the word hippie, but trying to just get that point across.
Like I think we're.
I think we're trying to be a bit more serious, is my takeaway.
We definitely look for a positive outlook.
Yeah.
Right?
So, you know, we never take the low road.
We always take the high road, even in the face of adversity from competition, right?
That also kind of leans into that open ecosystem kind of thing, too.
It's part of that.
So, you're not going to see us bad-mouthing a competitor or, you know, pushing down.
know, certainly we'll do comparatives.
But even then, you know, it's a very interesting thing.
So, when we look at the major competitors that we've had for a long time, most of them are, if not all of them, are private equity-owned, right?
They have a little bit different rules when it comes to what you can and cannot say and what you can and cannot verify and how you put numbers and metrics.
good thing.
It's a very you.
They're a little bit more fast and loose than we are, right?
We've got an answer to the SEC and a bunch of others, right?
So when it comes to that seriousness and that high road is really important for us, that positivity.
I think the idea that we talked about earlier about the partnership and not a vendor and in it with you kind of thing is also another vibe that we enjoy.
And, you know, I think, Matt, you kind of said it.
We are going into that cybersecurity space.
So that seriousness, that reliability, that trust, right?
Trust is a big part of it, is another vibe that we probably need to make sure that we continue to carry.
Yeah, 100%.
Like, we're selling to companies.
Like, I feel like some of the MSPs, they maybe resonate with some of that more fun language in the past.
But if we're trying to, you know, new brand messaging.
New market-ish, new audience.
We've to up-level it.
But to Joe's point, everyone's threat-mongering.
There is an aspect of that inherently with cybersecurity, but is there a more positive angle?
Which I think there is with us.
There is.
There's a future with us.
It's positive.
You know what mean?
Kind of going from cool uncle to more of a trusted advisor.
Yeah, there you go.
All right.
Last couple questions.
Is there any content you guys feel that is just completely missing from, you know, being able to tell the Enable story?
Or white papers, case studies, benchmarks, explainers?
Anything you guys kind of...
of...
Wish you had on your content side?
The only thing I can think of right now is the fact that for so long we've been thought of as a platform company.
And then we're now transitioning to that security piece.
But I feel like a lot of people, there's not enough brand awareness in the market still.
Everyone thinks that we're just an RMM company.
So I feel like if there's one thing I can, you know, we can push for is just more brand awareness and not just that too, but also the products themselves need more awareness.
I feel like we have incredible, incredible products.
And a lot of people don't understand what we're, what we cannot provide them, I guess, what we can provide.
So.
Yeah.
And I'd add into that also, you know, content for, so majority of our business is done direct, right?
Like people come by direct from us.
We have increased our, uh, our reseller distributor model.
We're, you know, putting a lot of bets behind that.
And I think they are lacking some content for themselves, like our sales team internally, to share with our resellers and our distributors as to why they should resell Enable's products.
You know, we're starting that journey, and we have some baseline to it, but I would think that if I asked those teams, they would say we need more content to share out.
Yeah, that end-user content that attracts mid-market, that attracts people is definitely a big one.

You know, some of the other things that we're seeing in our tactics or in tactics is that, you know, CPC, you know, non-branded search is maybe not producing as much as it should or has done in the past.
You know, because people are looking more for content, or the AI responses are giving them answers to things that they might have had, et cetera, et cetera.
So, you know, those...
Let's call them snackables, right?
Like the, hey, how do I help me get me this, give me a listicle of that, the best practices of these, right, are good starters that actually produce value.
We're kind of leaning towards more of those lately in our paid social and a few others is the value items.
I would say SEO is the big one for me, right?
At least it's like there's a zillion and one keywords, phrases, and topics that we should be covering, right?
So we've kind of launched, and you've probably heard this from Nick, but we've launched like some AI-based encyclopedia-type information.
But to be able to scale that out is probably the bigger challenge, right?
It's not that we don't know what those words are.
It's how do we scale and get the content going and drop it in.
And then...
And even sometimes review it, right?
So because we've got all these products out there and the size of our company, some of our SEO team are not product experts, right?
They're SEO experts.
And so there's still like this clunky process of, okay, we can generate some content, but we've got to review it, we've got to look at it, and then we've got to push it back out, right?
It can be cumbersome.
So those types of things can help really defer the dollars that we're spending and supplement the traffic, where we can then go invest or reinvest those things in other places, right?
And even on brand, right, we see competitors are, you know, bidding, you know, my brand level CPC doubled its cost from 2023 to now, right?
And that's because other competitors are out there bidding on our words, right?
Bidding on our company name or our product name.
And so without that extra SEO content, content saying,
Get it to other places and really cross-linking coming back without it where, you know, it reduces the effectiveness of those spend.
So SEO and GEO are really kind of key things that I want to, I would want to focus on.
That's what we're here for.

I like that somebody brought up the end user thing, too, because when I go back into my time, when we first, when we were SolarWinds, I think the only time that I ever understood end users knowing who we were was when we were breached.
And then they saw SolarWinds show up on their computers and they were like telling their MSPs to remove us, you know, like find somebody else.
We don't want this.
Like that's kind of like the only time that I think like an end user has ever really understood who we are or what we do.
And it was, it's interesting because it was an awful thing, but it's like, I don't think that end users know anything about what they're supposed to be doing and how.
They should be considering security on their end, you know?
So that's a really interesting, I think that's a really good one to kind of also focus on.
Yeah, and I completely agree with that.
Like there's even, like Datto as an example, some end users request Datto specifically in the NSP is forced to use them because they were seen as like the golden standard for the tools.

And a lot of competition too, like Acronis and Halo and a lot of these other vendors that we compete with in the space are doing things that are end user facing, like sponsoring a Formula One car so everyone can see Acronis or see Halo and everyone's like, okay, what's this company?
Then when they look them up, they see that it's a company that sells these tools just like us.
But again, we're in the shadows behind this curtain where now a lot of these other competitive, these competitors of ours are becoming end user facing.
Yeah, and we're purple and way cuter than those.
Just saying.
I agree.
The most important part, you're cute.
Cute praying colors.
we're way cuter.
Awesome.
Well, those are all the questions I have.
Is there anything that I didn't ask that you feel like we should know?
I know there's probably a lot, but anything in the last few minutes here?
That's a good question.
What about promos and stuff?
I feel like, is this something that you guys are going to need to know?
Pricing, promo plays, bio, like we do contract bio.
It's like all these different things that we do at the sales side.
Is that relevant or important?
And if it's not, it's fine.
Yeah, would probably say not.
Sure.
A lot of the content we do.
Yeah, too high in the funnel.
A lot of the stuff we do is editorial content.
Kind of.
I guess the theory that we have is to kind of turn you guys into a publisher so that you're consistently talking about what your audience wants to hear about, whether that's MSP or IT teams.

And so we'll continuously kind of find those topic clusters that are relevant and resonate with your audience and then try to put out as much as content possible to kind of meet them where they're at.
And that also helps from SEO perspective and then helps you guys show up in those LLM-generated answers, the generative engine optimization that we work on.
So that's at least the kind of crux of who we are and what we do.
Nice.
Wonderful.
Well, if there's nothing else, really appreciate the time from everyone on a Friday and talking me all through this.
So if we have any questions, we'll be sure to be in touch.
If those case studies pop up, we'd love to see them.
But other than that, appreciate the time.
Have a great weekend.
Thank you.
Thank you so much.
Bye.
Bye, everyone.
care.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Deep dive into N-able's product marketing, customer success, and sales strategies to inform content marketing efforts.](https://fathom.video/share/KxEnugPzggQft1p5xNw49MyAsd1xDWSb?tab=summary&timestamp=155.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [N-able is repositioning from IT management to cyber resilience, offering a full suite of "before, during, and after" attack solutions](https://fathom.video/share/KxEnugPzggQft1p5xNw49MyAsd1xDWSb?tab=summary&timestamp=452.0)
  - [Key differentiators: vendor-agnostic approach, best-in-class tools, partnership mentality, and focus on reducing customer labor costs](https://fathom.video/share/KxEnugPzggQft1p5xNw49MyAsd1xDWSb?tab=summary&timestamp=1075.0)
  - [Primary customer base is MSPs (85-90%), with growing focus on mid-market IT departments](https://fathom.video/share/KxEnugPzggQft1p5xNw49MyAsd1xDWSb?tab=summary&timestamp=2143.0)
  - [Content needs: improved brand awareness, end-user focused content, and SEO/keyword optimization](https://fathom.video/share/KxEnugPzggQft1p5xNw49MyAsd1xDWSb?tab=summary&timestamp=2918.0)

## Topics

### N-able's Repositioning Strategy

  - [Shifting from IT security/management to cyber resilience and cybersecurity](https://fathom.video/share/KxEnugPzggQft1p5xNw49MyAsd1xDWSb?tab=summary&timestamp=452.0)
  - [Acquired AdLumen for threat detection and response capabilities](https://fathom.video/share/KxEnugPzggQft1p5xNw49MyAsd1xDWSb?tab=summary&timestamp=540.0)
  - [Now offers full "before, during, and after" attack solution suite](https://fathom.video/share/KxEnugPzggQft1p5xNw49MyAsd1xDWSb?tab=summary&timestamp=584.0)
  - [Unique positioning as one of few vendors with comprehensive resilience offerings](https://fathom.video/share/KxEnugPzggQft1p5xNw49MyAsd1xDWSb?tab=summary&timestamp=584.0)
  - [Addressing growing cyber threats, especially in SMB market](https://fathom.video/share/KxEnugPzggQft1p5xNw49MyAsd1xDWSb?tab=summary&timestamp=706.0)

### Key Differentiators

  - [Vendor-agnostic approach, integrating with other solutions](https://fathom.video/share/KxEnugPzggQft1p5xNw49MyAsd1xDWSb?tab=summary&timestamp=933.0)
  - [Best-in-class tools across portfolio](https://fathom.video/share/KxEnugPzggQft1p5xNw49MyAsd1xDWSb?tab=summary&timestamp=933.0)
  - [Partnership mentality vs. vendor relationship](https://fathom.video/share/KxEnugPzggQft1p5xNw49MyAsd1xDWSb?tab=summary&timestamp=1199.0)
  - [Focus on reducing customer labor costs through automation and efficiency](https://fathom.video/share/KxEnugPzggQft1p5xNw49MyAsd1xDWSb?tab=summary&timestamp=1614.0)
  - [Strong customer success program (won Stevie award in 2024)](https://fathom.video/share/KxEnugPzggQft1p5xNw49MyAsd1xDWSb?tab=summary&timestamp=1275.0)
  - [Pricing models that encourage product usage (e.g., per node/agent pricing)](https://fathom.video/share/KxEnugPzggQft1p5xNw49MyAsd1xDWSb?tab=summary&timestamp=1383.0)

### Customer Base and Market Focus

  - [85-90% MSP clients, 10-15% mid-market IT departments](https://fathom.video/share/KxEnugPzggQft1p5xNw49MyAsd1xDWSb?tab=summary&timestamp=2143.0)
  - [Expanding focus on mid-market, especially with UEM solutions](https://fathom.video/share/KxEnugPzggQft1p5xNw49MyAsd1xDWSb?tab=summary&timestamp=2143.0)
  - [Channel-driven approach for AdLumen (distribution, resellers, VARs)](https://fathom.video/share/KxEnugPzggQft1p5xNw49MyAsd1xDWSb?tab=summary&timestamp=2343.0)
  - [Different sales strategies for MSP vs. mid-market (displacement vs. consolidation)](https://fathom.video/share/KxEnugPzggQft1p5xNw49MyAsd1xDWSb?tab=summary&timestamp=2013.0)

### Product Portfolio

  - [Unified Endpoint Management (UEM/RMM): Core control panel for MSPs](https://fathom.video/share/KxEnugPzggQft1p5xNw49MyAsd1xDWSb?tab=summary&timestamp=2254.0)
  - [Cove: Data protection and backup solution](https://fathom.video/share/KxEnugPzggQft1p5xNw49MyAsd1xDWSb?tab=summary&timestamp=1969.0)
  - [AdLumen: Managed Detection and Response (MDR)](https://fathom.video/share/KxEnugPzggQft1p5xNw49MyAsd1xDWSb?tab=summary&timestamp=1938.0)
  - [Emphasis on selling full suite, but customers often adopt incrementally](https://fathom.video/share/KxEnugPzggQft1p5xNw49MyAsd1xDWSb?tab=summary&timestamp=2203.0)

### Brand Voice and Messaging

  - [Shifting from "fun startup vibe" to more serious, trustworthy advisor](https://fathom.video/share/KxEnugPzggQft1p5xNw49MyAsd1xDWSb?tab=summary&timestamp=2693.0)
  - [Maintaining positive outlook and high-road approach vs. competitors](https://fathom.video/share/KxEnugPzggQft1p5xNw49MyAsd1xDWSb?tab=summary&timestamp=2765.0)
  - [Emphasizing partnership, reliability, and trust](https://fathom.video/share/KxEnugPzggQft1p5xNw49MyAsd1xDWSb?tab=summary&timestamp=2836.0)
  - [Balancing serious cybersecurity messaging with positive future outlook](https://fathom.video/share/KxEnugPzggQft1p5xNw49MyAsd1xDWSb?tab=summary&timestamp=2886.0)

### Content and Marketing Needs

  - [Increased brand awareness beyond RMM/platform perception](https://fathom.video/share/KxEnugPzggQft1p5xNw49MyAsd1xDWSb?tab=summary&timestamp=2950.0)
  - [End-user focused content to drive mid-market adoption](https://fathom.video/share/KxEnugPzggQft1p5xNw49MyAsd1xDWSb?tab=summary&timestamp=3026.0)
  - [SEO optimization for vast keyword opportunities](https://fathom.video/share/KxEnugPzggQft1p5xNw49MyAsd1xDWSb?tab=summary&timestamp=3084.0)
  - [Scalable content creation and review process](https://fathom.video/share/KxEnugPzggQft1p5xNw49MyAsd1xDWSb?tab=summary&timestamp=3084.0)
  - ["Snackable" content (listicles, best practices) for paid social](https://fathom.video/share/KxEnugPzggQft1p5xNw49MyAsd1xDWSb?tab=summary&timestamp=3026.0)
  - [Case studies showcasing full product portfolio impact](https://fathom.video/share/KxEnugPzggQft1p5xNw49MyAsd1xDWSb?tab=summary&timestamp=2415.0)

## Next Steps

  - [Develop content strategy focusing on cyber resilience messaging](https://fathom.video/share/KxEnugPzggQft1p5xNw49MyAsd1xDWSb?tab=summary&timestamp=2573.0)
  - [Create end-user focused content to improve brand awareness](https://fathom.video/share/KxEnugPzggQft1p5xNw49MyAsd1xDWSb?tab=summary&timestamp=3026.0)
  - [Optimize SEO efforts, targeting key industry keywords and phrases](https://fathom.video/share/KxEnugPzggQft1p5xNw49MyAsd1xDWSb?tab=summary&timestamp=3084.0)
  - [Produce case studies highlighting full product portfolio impact](https://fathom.video/share/KxEnugPzggQft1p5xNw49MyAsd1xDWSb?tab=summary&timestamp=2415.0)
  - [Develop content for resellers/distributors to support channel growth](https://fathom.video/share/KxEnugPzggQft1p5xNw49MyAsd1xDWSb?tab=summary&timestamp=2987.0)
